title: Coroutines should include well-defined exception behavior
description: When a regular function throws an exception, stack unwinding occurs. This applies to exceptions thrown within the function body or from an inner function call. C++20 introduced coroutines, a stackless method to invoke functions that can be suspended and resumed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Coroutines should include well-defined exception behavior (try/catch or unhandled_exception)
